title: Flutter Engine
objectives: Describe the engine's layers and threads
Explain how Flutter draws its own pixels
Understand the embedder and framework split

sections.markdown: The Flutter Engine (C++) provides the low-level runtime: the Dart VM, the graphics layer (Impeller/Skia), text layout, and platform plumbing. The Dart framework (widgets/material) runs on top of it.

Unlike React Native, Flutter does NOT use native UI widgets. It draws every pixel itself via its own graphics engine on a canvas the OS gives it. That's why Flutter UIs look identical across platforms and versions.

sections.code:
Your Dart app
  -> Flutter Framework (widgets, rendering) [Dart]
     -> Flutter Engine (Dart VM, Impeller/Skia, text) [C++]
        -> Embedder (Android/iOS/desktop host) -> OS surface

sections.markdown: The embedder is the platform-specific entry point that hosts the engine, provides a render surface, and pumps events. This layered design is how Flutter targets mobile, web, desktop and embedded from one codebase.

flashcards.back: C++ — it hosts the Dart VM, graphics (Impeller/Skia), and text layout.
No — it draws every pixel itself, so the UI is consistent across platforms.
The platform-specific host that runs the engine, gives it a surface, and delivers events.

quiz.explain: Flutter paints to its own canvas via the engine's graphics layer.

quiz.q: The engine is written mostly in…
quiz.explain: The engine is C++, hosting the Dart VM and graphics.
The raster thread turns layers into pixels via the GPU.

challenges.text: Diagram the engine/framework/embedder layers from memory.
Explain which thread each phase of a frame runs on.
Trace what happens from setState() to pixels on screen.
Build and run a custom engine/embedder for an embedded target (conceptual write-up).

interviewQuestions: What does the Flutter Engine provide?
How is Flutter different from React Native at the rendering level?
Name the engine threads and their roles.
How does one codebase target so many platforms?
